description: Adventure Maps, Multi-Digimon Party, Jogress and Full Roster Wiring - sixteen map backgrounds become places that accrue your steps, supply your battle opponents and hold the Digitama you can find; the save grows from one Digimon to a box of many with exactly one active and the rest frozen; two owned Digimon fuse by Jogress and hand one of their eggs back; the light button leaves the Digimon's play area; and the 780 playable Digimon that today have no evolution edge at all get wired into the graph. Source PRD: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md

Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-114.
acceptanceCriteria: The light button is a ToolbarItem(placement: .topBarLeading), mirroring the Dex book at .topBarTrailing (ContentView.swift:275)
LightButton is removed from LightLayer; LightLayer draws the scrim and nothing else
The scrim still covers ONLY the sprite slot, not the whole screen - US-112's behaviour is unchanged
The toolbar light button is never dimmed by the scrim, exactly like the Dex button
Tapping it still calls model.cycleLight() and its icon still reflects the current LightState
LightButtonLayout.inset is no longer subtracted from anything the sprite can reach
Existing light tests (LightSwitchView / Light) pass, updated for the new placement

Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot the main screen before and after this change on 41mm and 46mm; the sprite's reachable area is WIDER after, and no button overlaps the sprite at any walk offset
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Light button is now ToolbarItem(.topBarLeading), mirroring the Dex book; LightLayer draws the scrim only and LightButtonLayout.inset is gone. Build green, 1324 tests, 0 failures. Measured on 41mm and 46mm, pre- vs post-story builds: the lamp's old 24x24pt home in the slot's top-leading corner is now EMPTY (0 lit px over 150 frames, both screens); the new glyph clears the slot's top edge by 64px/69px, so no button can overlap the sprite at ANY walk offset; the sprite's box and the Dex button are pixel-identical before and after; US-112 is intact (sprite peak 255->38 = x0.15, chrome identical on and off); the toolbar lamp stays at peak 255 with the light out. CAVEAT ON AC6/AC8, see progress.txt: the sprite's REACHABLE area is not wider, because `inset` was never subtracted from the walk bound (it only placed the button); the walk bound reads device width and sprite size alone. What grew is the UNOBSTRUCTED area, by the button's whole 24x24pt footprint. Passed on the story's goal, which is met and measured. Separate pre-existing trap logged for US-115: -wanderDemo animates but does not translate on either simulator, identically in both builds.

userStories.title: The selected map draws behind the Digimon
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see where my Digimon is adventuring. Sixteen backgrounds already sit in Resources/Assets.xcassets (01_grassland ... 16_iceland) and nothing draws them. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-115.
acceptanceCriteria: New MapBackgroundView draws the selected map's asset behind the sprite slot ONLY - not behind the energy bars, the action row, or the toolbar
Opacity is a named constant in the range 0.30-0.50, default 0.35
Test: the opacity constant is >= 0.30 and <= 0.50, so a later edit that brightens it fails the suite
The image is .scaledToFill() and clipped to the sprite slot; it never grows layout in either axis
Under LightState dim/off the scrim covers the map background too - the background is INSIDE the scrimmed region, painted below the scrim
With no map selected the background is absent and layout is byte-for-byte what US-114 left

Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot the sprite over 01_grassland, 14_farmland and 16_iceland (the three brightest assets) and confirm the Digimon's pixels are still distinguishable from the background
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: MapBackgroundView draws the selected map at 0.35 inside a backgroundPreferenceValue off SpriteSlotBoundsKey - the same anchor the scrim uses - so it is behind the sprite and below the scrim by construction. Build green, 1331 tests (1324 + 7 new), 0 failures. MEASURED on 46mm, clean installs, pre- vs post-story builds (details in progress.txt): a map changes EXACTLY rows y153-259, x4-411, and nothing else on the 496-row screen, over all three of 01_grassland/14_farmland/16_iceland - so it is not behind the stats strip, the name line, the bars, the action row or the toolbar, and it grows layout in neither axis. On matched walk frames all 2916 of the sprite's pixels are BYTE-IDENTICAL with a map behind them, on all three maps: the map is strictly behind the Digimon. Scrim: the map band's mean luma 63.485 -> 31.721 at semi (x0.4997) -> 9.480 at off (x0.1493), exactly dimOpacity, while chrome above and below is identical to three decimals with the light on and out. Opacity recovered from the screen against a same-math reference render is 0.339/0.339/0.342 on the three assets, i.e. the authored 0.35. AC6: with no map selected the ONLY pixels differing from the US-114 build are the clock digits (x175-240 y39-62) - byte-for-byte otherwise. AC7: the sprite's DIMMEST pixel is luma 146.3 and the BRIGHTEST map pixel beside it is 88.4 across all three brightest assets, so the two ranges do not overlap at all. Seam for US-116/US-119: MainScreenModel.selectedMapAsset is nil for every real save today and is set only by the new DEBUG -mapDemo=<asset> flag; US-119 repoints it at PlayerProfile without touching the view.

userStories.title: Map catalog data file and model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the 16 maps as shipped data so tuning a map needs no code change - the same split evolutions.json and roster.json already use. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-116.
acceptanceCriteria: New Resources/maps.json, carried by the existing Resources folder reference in project.yml

New Sources/MapCatalog.swift with AdventureMap (id, displayName, assetName, tier, totalSteps, unlockedBy, opponentPool: [String], digitamaSlots: [DigitamaSlot]) and MapCatalog, loading it the way Roster.bundled does - fatalError on undecodable data, because a broken generated file is a broken build
All 16 maps present with assetName matching an imageset that EXISTS in Resources/Assets.xcassets - verified with ls, not assumed

Tiers, order and totalSteps match the FR-6 table exactly: 01_grassland 3000, 14_farmland 5000, 02_river 8000, 08_jungle 10000, 09_lake 12000, 04_desert 16000, 07_mountains 18000, 16_iceland 20000, 03_ocean 22000, 05_wasteland 26000, 06_industrial 28000, 13_factory_town 30000, 10_city_dusk 32000, 15_dungeon 38000, 11_city_night 42000, 12_cyberpunk 50000
unlockedBy forms a single linear chain: map 1 has none, every other map names its predecessor
Test: MapCatalog.bundled decodes and holds exactly 16 maps
Test: every assetName resolves to a non-nil image at runtime
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Resources/maps.json (16 maps) + Sources/MapCatalog.swift (AdventureMap, DigitamaSlot, MapCatalog) in the Roster.bundled shape - hand-written decoder, fatalError on undecodable data. Build green, 1348 tests (1331 + 17 new), 0 failures. BUNDLING: no project.yml change was needed and none was made; verified the file is really in the built app - DigiVPet.app/maps.json, 28644 bytes - and every test reads it through MapCatalog.load() off Bundle.main. ASSETS: all 16 assetNames resolve to a non-nil UIImage at runtime (test), and ls confirmed the 16 imagesets in Resources/Assets.xcassets. FR-6: the test spells the table out verbatim rather than deriving it from the file - order, tier and totalSteps all match, plus monotonic totals and non-decreasing tiers. CHAIN: map 1 has no unlockedBy, every other names its predecessor, exactly one starting map, and walking the chain from the start reaches all 16. SCOPE NOTE, deliberate: opponentPool and digitamaSlots are AUTHORED here, not left empty. No later story authors them and US-121/122/128 all consume them, so empty lists would have made those stories untestable. 170 opponent ids across the 16 pools and all 57 Digitama were resolved from DISPLAY NAMES against roster.json by a throwaway script (/tmp/us116/build_maps.py, not committed) that hard-errors on an unknown name, an ambiguous name, or a dexOnly entry - so no id here was typed by hand or invented. Caught by it: 'Rapidmon' is two roster entries (rapidmon Perfect, rapidmon_armor Armor-Hybrid) and had to be pinned by id. Digitama follow FR-12's suggested assignment exactly; a test asserts all 57 are placed and none twice. Conditions scale with tier (FR-13) and every one has a non-empty hint and a known ConditionMetric, asserted by a test. ONE JUDGEMENT CALL: FR-13 names health.handwashing for 06_industrial/Pulse, which is in the family that is typically empty on real hardware - authored as FR-13 asks but PAIRED with health.steps so it is never the sole gate, per US-128's rule. Every other slot avoids that family entirely. US-117 now has real data to validate.

userStories.title: Map catalog validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need bad map data to fail loudly in a test rather than quietly at runtime, the way EvolutionGraphValidator already does for the evolution graph. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-117.
acceptanceCriteria: New Sources/MapCatalogValidator.swift, in the same shape as EvolutionGraphValidator (returns findings, does not throw)
Rejects an assetName with no matching imageset
Rejects an opponentPool id absent from the roster
Rejects an opponentPool id whose roster entry is dexOnly - a dexOnly Digimon has no animated sheet and can never be an opponent
Rejects a digitamaSlots id that is not a roster entry at Stage.digitama
Rejects an unlockedBy that names no map, and rejects any cycle in the unlock chain
Rejects a DigitamaSlot condition with a blank hint
Test: the validator reports ZERO findings over the shipped maps.json
Test: each rejection rule fires on a hand-built bad fixture
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/MapCatalogValidator.swift in the EvolutionGraphValidator shape: MapValidationError (8 cases) + MapCatalog.validate(roster:assetExists:) returning findings in map order with the unlock cycles last, never throwing, plus MapCatalog.assetExists() as the injectable real check. Build green, 1363 tests (1348 + 15 new), 0 failures. EVERY REJECTION RULE FIRES ON A FIXTURE, each one the sound two-map catalog with exactly one thing broken so the reported error can only be the break: missingAsset (unknown imageset, and separately an empty assetName through the REAL check), unknownOpponent, dexOnlyOpponent, unknownDigitama, notADigitama (a Child in a slot), unknownUnlockedBy, unlockCycle (a 3-map loop, a map that unlocks itself, and a loop three maps hang off reported ONCE), emptyConditionHint (whitespace-only). A control test asserts the sound catalog reports []. AC8 run against the REAL roster and the REAL asset catalog rather than stubs - MapCatalog.load().validate() reports zero findings over the shipped 16 maps, 170 opponent ids and 57 Digitama slots - and a companion test proves the real assetExists is a check at all ('01_grassland' true, '17_moonbase' false, '' false), so the zero cannot be a stub saying yes to everything. Ordering choice worth knowing: a slot's blank-hint findings are collected BEFORE its roster lookup, so a broken slot id does not hide them and the author fixes both in one run - the same reason EvolutionGraphValidator checks conditions before the edge target. Cycle detection walks up from EVERY map, not just the roots: a cycle nothing hangs off is invisible to a walk from the starting map, which is exactly what the US-116 reachability test cannot see.

userStories.title: Steps accrue to the selected map
userStories.description: As a player, I want the steps I walk to count toward the map I chose, so a map is a place I make progress in rather than a backdrop. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-118.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-map recorded steps and a finishedAt date persist across launches
ONLY steps read while that map is selected accrue to it; steps banked under a different map never move

Accrual is credited from the SAME already-deduplicated step deltas the energy path uses (HealthMetricReader / MetricLedger), so walking 1000 steps credits the map 1000 and not 2000 when two reads overlap
Test: two overlapping fixture reads credit the map once
Test: switching maps mid-day leaves already-credited steps where they were
A map is marked finished the first time recorded >= totalSteps, with a timestamp; crossing the total again does not re-fire
Recorded steps are NOT capped at the total - the counter keeps climbing past a finish
The clock and the health reader are injected; no test waits real time and no test queries live HealthKit
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/MapProgress.swift: a single global @Model MapProgress (selectedMapId + [mapId: steps] + [mapId: finishedAt]) in the MetricLedger dictionary shape, plus MapStepCreditor beside EnergyCreditor/MetricCreditor. GameStore gains MapProgress to the schema and loadOrCreateMapProgress(); MainScreenModel gains maps:, mapProgress, selectMap(_:) and creditMapSteps. Build green, 1386 tests (1363 + 23 new), 0 failures, every new test also observed passing on its own. DE-DUPLICATION IS SHARED, NOT COPIED (AC3): the delta comes from a new MetricLedger.claim(_:dayTotal:now:calendar:) which is now the ONE place the day-roll and the max(0, total - baseline) rule live - MetricCreditor was refactored onto it, so the map spends the same delta any later metric consumer will, rather than keeping a second baseline that can drift. The step total reaches it through a new HealthEnergySource.dayReadings, which keeps ONE read in both shapes: byEnergyType for energy, quantities[.steps] for the map. Reading HealthKit a second time would have been two answers about one day. AC4 (1000 steps read twice credits 1000) and the growth case (1000 then 1600 credits 1600) both pass through the real refresh. AC2/AC5: a map is credited only what is read WHILE it is selected - steps banked before any map was chosen are dropped rather than parked, and switching mid-day leaves 600 on the first map and puts only the later 400 on the second. Deliberate: with no map selected the delta is claimed and DROPPED, because not claiming it would let a selection made at 18:00 retroactively bank the whole day. FINISH (AC6/AC7): stamped the first time recorded >= totalSteps, boundary pinned in both directions (999 no, 1000 yes), a second and third crossing keep the FIRST stamp, and the counter runs to 4500 past a 1000-step map. Clock and reader injected throughout; no test waits real time or touches live HealthKit. Progress outlives the Digimon - a test rebirths through GameStore and the 1000 steps and the selection are still there. NOT IN SCOPE BUT FOUND, see progress.txt: MetricCreditor has NEVER been wired into refresh (US-058 deferred it to a story that was never written), so stage/lifetime metric totals are still zero in the shipped app and the 78 window:stage health conditions in evolutions.json cannot fire. Untouched here on purpose - wiring it changes evolution eligibility across the whole game and is its own story. US-123 folds these three fields onto PlayerProfile; the shape was chosen to make that a move, not a rewrite.

userStories.title: Map list screen
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see every map, my progress on it, and which ones are still locked. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-119.
acceptanceCriteria: New MapListView, pushed onto the existing NavigationStack so it keeps a back button
One row per map in tier order showing name, the map asset as a small thumbnail, and progress
Progress renders exactly as '1222 / 25000' - space, slash, space, no abbreviation and no rounding
A finished map shows a finished mark; the selected map shows a selected mark; the two marks are visually different from each other
A locked map shows a lock, hides its Digitama and opponent pool, and states its unlock condition in one line: 'Finish <previous map name>'
Tapping a locked map does NOT change the selection
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot MapListView on 41mm and 46mm with map 16 finished, so the progress text is at its widest (5-digit / 5-digit) - nothing truncates
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/MapListView.swift: MapListRow (a pure value built from the catalog + MapProgress), MapListMarks, MapListSelector and MapListView, pushed onto ContentView's existing NavigationStack beside the Dex so it keeps its back button. MainScreenModel gains mapRows. Build green, 1416 tests (1386 + 30 new), 0 failures, all 30 also observed passing on their own. AC3 spelled exactly: progressText is string interpolation, NOT a NumberFormatter - a formatter is locale-dependent and would render '25,000' in en_US and '25.000' in de_DE for a criterion that names one exact string; tests pin '1222 / 25000' and assert no comma, no dot, no non-breaking space. AC4: the finished mark (checkmark.seal.fill, green) and the selected mark (figure.walk.circle.fill, orange) differ in BOTH glyph and tint, asserted pairwise, and a row can carry both at once - which is why one channel could not do the job. isFinished reads the STAMP, not recorded >= total, so a map retuned longer in a later build stays finished. AC5: locked = predecessor not finished; a locked row's contents is nil (Digitama and opponent pool hidden), its unlockLine is 'Finish <previous displayName>', and over the shipped chain all 15 locked rows name a real map by display name. AC6: MapListSelector.selection(tapping:current:) hands `current` straight back for a locked row - not nil, since 'no change' and 'no map' are different answers - and the view also disables the button. SCREENSHOTS on 41mm (Series 9, watchOS 10.4) and 46mm (Series 11, watchOS 26.4), four states each: map 16 finished reads '50000 / 50000' IN FULL on both, no truncation and no ellipsis, plus a mixed state (both marks) and a locked state (lock glyph, dimmed row, 'Finish City Dusk', no eggs/foes line). THE SCREENSHOT CAUGHT A REAL BUG, see progress.txt: the -mapListDemo seed was not idempotent, so a second launch double-credited and map 16 read '100000 / 50000'; the seed now clears first. Incidental proof from that run: even a 6-digit/5-digit figure does not truncate on 41mm. New DEBUG flags -mapListDemo / -mapListPartialDemo / -mapListBottomDemo / -mapProgressResetDemo, the last being the inverse the Codebase Patterns rule demands since the first two write to the save; both simulator containers were left reset and photographed clean. US-120's strip pushes MapListView(rows: model.mapRows) { model.selectMap($0) } - the seam is already what that story wants.

userStories.title: Map strip on the main screen, and switching maps
userStories.description: As a player, I want to reach the maps in one tap and see where I am without leaving the main screen. The toolbar has only two usable slots on watchOS and US-114 took the leading one, so this goes above the energy bars rather than into the toolbar or the action row. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-120.
acceptanceCriteria: One thin row sits directly above the energy bars holding two controls: a wide leading NavigationLink to MapListView labelled with the selected map's name and 'recorded / total', and a trailing icon button that opens the party screen (US-126)
Until US-126 lands the trailing button may be present and disabled, but it must not be a dead tap target that looks live
The row costs at most one line of height
The sprite slot is NOT smaller than US-114 left it - measured against a US-114 screenshot on 41mm. Making the Digimon smaller than US-114 made it is a regression and fails this story
Selecting a map in MapListView persists the selection, updates the background from US-115 and the strip's text, and pops back to the main screen
With no map selected the strip names the first map as a prompt; the game stays fully playable and nothing is gated on having chosen a map
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot the main screen on 41mm and 46mm - the strip is readable, nothing truncates, and both controls are tappable without hitting each other
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PASSING. The previous iteration left AC4 as the only failure and called it a layout-budget decision; this iteration took the trade it named and MEASURED the result. The strip's ~13.5pt is now paid for out of the chrome around it, five trims: ContentView's VStack spacing 1->0 (5.0pt over five gaps), the name/stage line 12->9pt (3.6), StatsStrip's values 11->9pt (2.4, matching the 9pt labels already beside them), ActionControls' .padding(.top, 2) (2.0), EnergyBarsView's bar-row spacing 2->1 (1.0). Build green, 1436 tests (1435 + 1 new), 0 failures, the new test also observed passing on its own. AC4 MET AND MEASURED, pre- vs post-story, both watch sizes, fresh install, identical script and identical launch command (-healthAnswered -noNotificationPrompt -battleAffordableDemo -mapDemo=01_grassland vs =none). The baseline is a real build of 858fa43 (US-119, the last commit before the strip) made in a git worktree, and it reproduced the previous iteration's numbers to the point - 49.0pt on 41mm, 64.0pt on 46mm - which is what makes the comparison trustworthy. SLOT: 41mm 49.0 -> 49.5pt, 46mm 64.0 -> 64.0pt. NOT SMALLER on either, which is what AC4 asks. SPRITE, measured directly as ink on black inside the slot: 41mm 78x72px in BOTH builds, byte-for-byte the same size at a different walk offset; 46mm 104px wide in BOTH (the 104 vs 96 height is the walk1/walk2 confound the Codebase Patterns warn about - width is the honest signal). So the Digimon is scale 3 on 41mm and scale 4 on 46mm before and after, unchanged. The name line no longer runs through the Digimon either - the defect the previous iteration photographed: sprite ink ends y221 and the name starts y233 on 41mm (6.0pt clear), y266 and y279 on 46mm (6.5pt clear). A MEASUREMENT BUG WORTH KNOWING, and it nearly produced a false pass in the other direction: diffing -mapDemo=<asset> against -mapDemo=none on 'rows that differ at all' reports the slot as 72.0pt/87.5pt, because =none ALSO flips the strip to its prompt state (grey folded-map glyph for orange walk glyph) and nudges the name row, so those two text rows differ for reasons that are not the map. The map band is FULL WIDTH (344 of 352 px on 41mm) and text rows differ over at most half of it, so requiring >=75% of the width to differ isolates the true band; it comes out contiguous on all four shots. AC7 re-verified on the new build with -mapStripWidestDemo since the fonts around the strip moved: 41mm glyph x7-19, 'Cyberpunk' x31-137, '50000' x147-202, '/' x210-216, '50000' x224-279, an 18.5pt CLEAR GAP, party icon x317-343, 4.5pt right margin; both five-digit runs are the full 56px with no ellipsis, and 'Cyberpunk' is 107px on BOTH screens so minimumScaleFactor(0.7) never fired on the narrow one. US-115 is intact: the map band is exactly the slot, contiguous, and ends before the name row on both sizes. NEW GUARD: MainScreenTypography names the two font sizes the trim spent, because they are now load-bearing - the 41mm slot has 0.5pt of slack over a 16pt quantum, so a point of font silently costs the Digimon a third of its size - and MainScreenLayoutTests holds them at <= 9. Both simulator containers were erased afterwards; -mapStripWidestDemo writes to the save. PREVIOUS ITERATION'S NOTE, kept because the reasoning still stands: NOT PASSING, and the failure is AC4 alone. Sources/MapStripView.swift (MapStrip, MapStripMarks, MapStripLayout, MapStripView) + MainScreenModel.mapStrip + one row in ContentView above the energy bars. Build green, 1435 tests (1416 + 19 new), 0 failures, all 19 also observed passing on their own. AC1/AC2/AC3/AC5/AC6/AC7 all observed passing - see below. AC4 FAILS AND CANNOT BE MET AS THE SCREEN IS BUILT, measured rather than argued: THE SPRITE SLOT SITS AT EXACTLY THE SPRITE-SCALE BOUNDARY ON BOTH WATCH SIZES. Pre-story, hatched Child, map behind: the slot is 49.0pt on 41mm (SpriteScale.fitting floors 49/16 to 3, i.e. a 48pt Digimon, 1.0pt of slack) and 64.0pt on 46mm (floors 64/16 to 4, a 64pt Digimon, 0.0pt of slack). So ANY row of ANY height costs a whole scale step. This one costs 13.5pt on 41mm and 14.0pt on 46mm, and the result is measured: 41mm slot 49.0 -> 35.5pt and the Digimon 48 -> 32pt; 46mm slot 64.0 -> 50.0pt and the Digimon 64 -> 48pt. On a FRESH INSTALL - the state AC4 names, 'measured against a US-114 screenshot' - the Digimon does NOT shrink, because at 27.5pt (41mm) / 42.5pt (46mm) the slot is already below SpriteScale.minimum and the sprite is at 32.0pt in both builds, byte-identical placeholder boxes x144-207/x176-239; but the slot still loses 13.5/14.0pt and the sprite's overflow grows until the name line runs straight through the Digimon on 41mm, which is photographed. Both readings of AC4 fail. THE HEIGHT HAS TO COME FROM SOMEWHERE AND EVERY CANDIDATE WAS MEASURED, see progress.txt: (a) the bottom safe area is 26pt on 41mm / 36pt on 46mm and is genuinely unused - proved by an .ignoresSafeArea(.container, edges: .bottom) probe build, which gave the slot 49->75pt and 64->100pt - but claiming it slides the action row's five 30pt circles to within 12pt of the display's bottom edge, inside the corner arc, which is what that inset is for; (b) shrinking the surrounding chrome adds up to about 14pt (name 12->9pt 3.6, stats 11->9pt 2.4, ActionControls top padding 2, VStack spacing 1->0 5, bars spacing 1) but that is a screen-wide legibility regression traded for one row, and unrequested; (c) merging the strip into the name/stage row does not fit - at its widest the strip's own ink already runs x7-344 of 352 on 41mm. NEXT ITERATION: this is a layout-budget problem, not a map problem. Either take AC4's trade explicitly (accept a one-step smaller Digimon, which is what is committed) or free a full line first. The strip itself is done and measured; only the height question is open.

userStories.title: Map detail shows opponents and Digitama with progressive hints
userStories.description: As a player, I want to know what lives in a map and what I must do to find its eggs - told the same way the Dex tells me about an evolution. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-121.
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping a map row opens a detail view listing its opponent pool grouped by stage, and its Digitama slots
A Digitama the player has never owned draws as a '?' - the same choice DexCell already makes for an undiscovered Digimon (DexView.swift:191), a '?' and NOT a silhouette
Each '?' slot lists its conditions through ConditionReveal.line(for:in:), so a far-off condition reads vague and a nearly-met one reads specific - identical wording to the Dex's evolution hints
A Digitama the player owns or has owned draws its real sprite and name with no hint rows
A slot whose conditions are ALL currently met but which has not dropped yet is marked as ready
A locked map has no reachable detail view
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot an unlocked map's detail showing a mix of '?' slots and revealed slots on 46mm
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/MapDetailView.swift: MapDetail (a pure value built from the catalog, the roster, the Dex and the player's counters) + MapDetailView, pushed from MapListView with navigationDestination(item:). Build green, 1466 tests (1436 + 30 new), 0 failures, all 30 also observed passing on their own. AC1 SCREENSHOTTED on 46mm: the pool draws grouped, with the 'Child' heading between Nyaromon/Tokomon and Agumon/Patamon - grouping, not merely ordering. Group order is Stage.allCases (the ladder, side branch last) rather than ladderIndex, because Armor-Hybrid has no rung to sort on; within a group the authored order stands, since that is the order US-122 will band. AC2/AC3/AC4/AC5 ALL ON ONE 46mm SCREENSHOT, which is what -mapDetailSlotsDemo exists for: Agu Digitama with its real sprite and name and NO hint rows; a '?' marked 'Ready to find' (sparkles, yellow) with a green-checked 'Walk 2,000 steps in a day'; and a second '?' with an open circle beside 'Fight one battle'. AC3 is not merely the same WORDING as the Dex - the view draws these with ConditionHintRow, the Dex's own row type, made non-private for this; a second implementation could drift a word and the player would be reading two promises about one ConditionReveal output. AC4's 'no hint rows' is enforced on the VALUE (a revealed slot's conditions list is empty), so there is nothing for the view to draw even if it tried. AC5 is !revealed && ConditionReveal.allMet: an egg already owned is one that HAS dropped, so it is never 'ready', and a test pins that against an unowned slot ready on the same context. AC6: MapDetail.make returns NIL for a locked row - not a stripped detail - so there is nothing to push, and the row's button is disabled as well. NAVIGATION CHANGED, deliberately and it is the one thing worth review: US-119 made a row tap select-and-pop; AC1 says a tap opens the detail, so travelling moved one step deeper into a 'Travel here' button on the detail, which selects through MapListSelector and drops BOTH levels. Everything US-120 AC5 asked of a selection still holds of it (persisted, background, strip, ends on the main screen); what changed is that it takes two taps and shows the player what is in a map first. NOT TAP-VERIFIED: simctl has no tap command, so the two-level pop is reasoned, not photographed - the failure mode if SwiftUI honours only one of the two is landing on the map list, which is a degradation and not a hang. Reveal source is the DEX (GameStore.dexIds), which is written when an egg is handed over or hatched and survives death and rebirth - exactly the span 'owns or has owned' names; US-127's held-Digitama set will refine it. New DEBUG flags -mapDetailDemo / -mapDetailSlotsDemo / -mapDetailFoesDemo; all three seed through LITERALS (a ConditionContext and a discovery set unioned at read time) and write NOTHING to the save, so unlike US-119's they need no inverse. The 46mm container was erased afterwards anyway. Also caught by the first screenshot: a withheld row drew '?' twice, tile and title, which read as a rendering fault - the title is now drawn only for a revealed slot.

userStories.title: Opponents come from the map and scale with progress
userStories.description: As a player, I want tougher opponents the further I get into a map. OpponentPicker currently draws from the whole roster (Battle.swift:191). Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-122.
acceptanceCriteria: OpponentPicker picks from the SELECTED map's opponentPool rather than the whole roster
The stage band is chosen by progress ratio p = recorded / total, clamped to the map's own tier band: p < 0.25 the pool's lowest band, < 0.50 second, < 0.75 third, >= 0.75 the pool's highest band
A finished map (p >= 1) still picks from the highest band and never above it
A map whose pool has no member in the computed band falls back to the NEAREST populated band rather than returning nil
The pick is still seeded by an injected RandomNumberGenerator, so the band is testable without flakiness
With no map selected, behaviour is exactly today's roster-wide pick - no regression
Test: one per band (4), plus the empty-band fallback, plus the no-map-selected path
Existing battle tests pass unchanged
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/MapOpponents.swift: MapOpponentBand (pure band arithmetic, no generator at all) + BattleMatchmaker.choose(in:roster:playerId:map:recorded:using:). Build green, 1496 tests (1466 + 30 new), 0 failures, all 30 also observed passing on their own. THE FIRST BUILD FOUND A REAL COLLISION AND IT IS THE ONE THING TO KNOW HERE: a map's opponentPool is authored and validated against the ROSTER (1025 entries, US-116/US-117), but BattleMatchmaker resolves against the EvolutionGraph, which authors only 88 nodes. Resolving a pool through the graph dropped nearly every opponent every map names and handed the fight straight back to the roster-wide pick - US-122 doing nothing while its tests passed. Caught because the shipped-catalog sweep asserts the drawn id is IN the map's pool, and 11_city_night drew ogremon. MapOpponentBand.nodes now resolves graph-node-first, roster-entry-promoted-to-a-node otherwise; an opponent needs a name, a stage and a sheet, not outgoing edges. A promoted node carries an EMPTY line, so MoveCatalog and ElementCatalog miss lineDefaults and fall through to their stage/keyword tiers - which is the honest answer for a Digimon in no tree - and the two element lookups in MainScreenModel moved from type(for:in:graph) to type(forId:line:) off the node in hand, identical for on-graph opponents and strictly better for off-graph ones. BANDS: index() is the literal quartile, pinned from BOTH sides at all three boundaries so an accidental <= fails. The tier clamp is a rung table (t1 2...3 Baby II-Child, up to t5 6...6 Ultimate alone) and does real work - 13_factory_town carries one Adult among its Perfects and Ultimates, which is dropped rather than being fought at the start of a tier 4 map. Within the clamped pool the 4 bands INTERPOLATE over the rungs the pool actually spans, so index 0 IS the pool's lowest band and index 3 IS its highest whatever it holds: a 4-rung pool gives a stage per band, and the 2-rung shape every shipped map has splits at halfway. AC4's nearest-populated fallback is tested three ways (nearer below, nearer above, and a tie - which breaks DOWNWARD, and that tie-break is what keeps AC3's 'never above it' true when the top band itself is empty). AC6 IS ASSERTED AGAINST THE OLD CALL, not against a remembered list: with map nil the new signature returns the same node and the same rolled power at 50 seeds and leaves the generator in the same place, so the battle that draws from it afterwards resolves identically too. The strength roll moved into BattleMatchmaker.rolled so the map path cannot drift easier than the roster path. AC8 held LITERALLY - not one existing test file was touched, git diff shows only Battle.swift and MainScreenModel.swift changed. SHIPPED DATA verified two ways: the test sweeps all 16 maps at 6 ratios x 5 seeds (always from the map's own pool, never above its tier, the band never falls as progress rises, 01_grassland starts on Baby II and 12_cyberpunk on Ultimate), and a throwaway Python reimplementation of the rule off maps.json + roster.json reproduces the same table map-for-map - every map steps up at halfway, t5 is one band throughout. Also carried the map into the DEBUG demoBattleSeed search, since that replays exactly what battle() is about to draw and that now depends on where the player is.

userStories.title: PlayerProfile holds what outlives a Digimon, and the existing save migrates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need somewhere for map progress and lifetime energy to live that is not tied to one Digimon. This is the riskiest story in the wave: a migration that loses a live player's pet is not recoverable. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-123.
acceptanceCriteria: New @Model PlayerProfile holding: carried lifetimeEnergy, selected map id, per-map recorded steps and finishedAt, and the set of Digitama ids ever owned
PlayerProfile is added to GameStore.schema - a @Model missing from it is silently never saved
lifetimeEnergy moves OFF GameState onto PlayerProfile; every read of it goes through the profile
GameStore gains loadOrCreateProfile(), which is the only way in, like loadOrCreate

MIGRATION: a store written by the CURRENT build opens with its GameState intact and its lifetimeEnergy copied onto a newly created PlayerProfile - stage, energy, care mistakes, poop, battle record, light state and sickness all survive

Test: write a store in the pre-migration shape, reopen it through the new GameStore, and assert every one of those fields. A test that constructs the NEW shape directly does not satisfy this criterion
EnergyLedger and MetricLedger stay single global records and are not touched
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/PlayerProfile.swift: @Model PlayerProfile (lifetimeEnergy, selectedMapId, recorded, finishedAt, ownedDigitamaIds) + MapStepCreditor moved onto it; GameStore gains loadOrCreateProfile(roster:) and loses loadOrCreateMapProgress. Build green, 1510 tests (1496 + 14 new), 0 failures, all 14 also observed passing on their own. THE MIGRATION IS TESTED AGAINST A REAL FILE THE PREVIOUS BUILD WROTE, which is the one thing worth knowing here: before touching a line of code this iteration ran a throwaway test on the US-122 commit that saved a fully-populated store, and that file is committed as Tests/Fixtures/pre-us123.store (96KB, bundled into the test target by xcodegen with no project.yml change). Four tests open it - and it is genuinely the old shape: sqlite3 shows ZGAMESTATE and ZMAPPROGRESS and NO ZPLAYERPROFILE. AC5/AC6 observed: greymon comes back Adult with stageEnergy 11/22/33/44, birth and stage dates, 2 care mistakes, hunger 3, strength 17, sick with its sickSince, 5-6 battle record, 4 poops, light off - and 55/66/77/88 on a profile that did not exist when the file was written. A fifth test runs the SAME file through MainScreenModel.start(), the app's own path, and gets phase .playing, greymon, the same 55/66/77/88 through model.lifetimeEnergy and the migrated 02_river selection driving US-115's background. AC3 WITH ONE DELIBERATE EXCEPTION, stated plainly: the Swift property is gone from GameState's API - private, renamed nowhere, exposed only as the read-only legacyLifetimeEnergy the migration drains - but THE COLUMN STAYS. Deleting it would delete a live player's earnings with no second chance to read them, and renaming is no safer: SwiftData FLATTENS an EnergyTotals into one column per field named BY POSITION (ZSTRENGTH1, ZSTRENGTH2...), verified by opening the fixture in sqlite3, so a rename is a schema change that has to be exactly right for the one value nothing may lose. Every read in the game goes through the profile: EnergyCreditor.credit gained a profile: parameter and tops it up beside stageEnergy, GameState.battlePower and memorial(displayName:) take the total as a parameter, and MainScreenModel.lifetimeEnergy is the single place the 'no profile yet' answer is decided. MAP PROGRESS MIGRATES TOO rather than being dropped: MapProgress survives as a drain-only legacy @Model still in the schema, and loadOrCreateProfile adopts its three fields and DELETES the record - asserted, including that no MapProgress row is left. ownedDigitamaIds is seeded from the DEX filtered through the roster's stages, because the Dex has recorded every egg the moment it was handed over since US-016 - so 'ever owned' is a fact the old save knows, not a guess (the fixture's Dex has greymon and agu_digitama; only agu_digitama comes across). resetGame now wipes the profile's total explicitly and rebirth reads-then-restores it, so both pre-story behaviours hold with the value on its new home; a test pins that a rebirth keeps 111/222/333/444 AND the 900 steps walked. AC7 asserted after the migration: one EnergyLedger, one MetricLedger, untouched. The migration is once-only, tested by playing on and reopening (1055, not 55 again).

userStories.title: The store can hold many Digimon, one of them active
userStories.description: As a developer, I need more than one saved Digimon. GameStore.loadOrCreate fetches .first today, so there is exactly one. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-124.
acceptanceCriteria: GameState gains isActive: Bool and the store may hold many records
GameStore gains activeState(), allStates() and activate(_:); savedState() returns the ACTIVE record
Migration from US-123 sets isActive = true on the single existing record
activate(_:) freezes the previously active record and activates the new one in ONE saved transaction - a crash part-way can never leave zero active or two active
Test, asserted directly: at most one GameState has isActive == true, after a fresh start, after an activate, and after a failed activate
resetGame and rebirth still work and still operate on the active record
Every existing caller of savedState() still compiles and still sees the Digimon it saw before
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GameState gains a private isActiveStorage: Bool? behind a computed isActive; GameStore gains allStates(), activeState(), activate(_:) and GameStoreError.stateNotInStore, and savedState() is now activeState(). Build green, 1533 tests (1510 + 23 new), 0 failures, all 23 also observed passing on their own. THE INVARIANT LIVES IN THE STORE, NOT ON THE RECORD, and that is the design decision worth reviewing: 'exactly one active' is a fact about the whole store, so flipping one record's flag on leaves two active unless another is flipped off in the same transaction. activate(_:) writes EVERY record it fetched - not just the two it is moving between - then calls save() ONCE, so the file goes straight from 'A is out' to 'B is out'; a test that hand-corrupts a store into two active and then activates a third gets back exactly one. AC5's third case is a REAL failure and not a contrived throw: activate is handed a live, inserted GameState belonging to a DIFFERENT GameStore, which is exactly what a party screen caching a row across stores would do. It is refused on persistentModelID BEFORE anything is mutated, so nothing to roll back and nothing reaches disk - asserted both in memory and through a reopen. A save() that throws after the flips is caught and every flag put back, which is the untestable half stated in the code. AC3 PROVED AGAINST A REAL PRE-BOX FILE, Tests/Fixtures/pre-us123.store, and then CHECKED IN SQLITE3: the committed fixture has NO ZISACTIVESTORAGE COLUMN AT ALL, and after loadOrCreateProfile the row reads greymon|1, while a box written by this build reads agu_digitama|1, gabu_digitama|0. So the stamp is a real write and not the default reading back. TWO MECHANISMS ON PURPOSE, because one is not enough: the migration stamp only fires on a store that has no profile yet, and a player who ran the US-123 build ALREADY HAS ONE - loadOrCreateProfile returns early and never reaches it. So nil ALSO reads as ACTIVE, which is the safe direction: the only record that can carry a nil is the single Digimon of a pre-box store, and reading it as frozen would hand that player a fresh egg and leave their Digimon invisible. A test opens the fixture and calls savedState() WITHOUT migrating, to pin exactly that. The stamp is guarded on states.count == 1 because it is a migration and not a repair. AC6/AC7: resetGame still deletes the WHOLE box (a total wipe means all of them) and its fresh egg is born active; rebirth still carries lifetimeEnergy and ends with one out; loadOrCreate returns the ACTIVE record out of a box where it is not the first, which is the case that would have wiped two Digimon if it had fallen through to resetGame. ADDED DEFENCE, not asked for: a box with records but NOTHING active adopts the oldest instead of falling through to resetGame - nothing here can produce that state, but the cost of being wrong is deleting Digimon the player still owns. GameState.init gained isActive: Bool = true, so every existing call site is untouched and US-126 is the first caller to pass false. Only Sources/GameState.swift and Sources/GameStore.swift changed; not one existing test file was edited.

userStories.title: Frozen Digimon do not hunger, age, or evolve
userStories.description: As a player, I want the Digimon I put away to be exactly as I left them. Every hunger, sickness and ageing reading is derived from stored Dates, so freezing is a clock offset rather than a paused timer - that is the only approach that does not mean touching each of them. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-125.
acceptanceCriteria: An inactive GameState accrues no hunger, no care mistakes, no sickness, no ageing toward death, no poop, and is never evaluated for evolution
Freezing records frozenSince; activating adds the elapsed span to a stored frozenDuration and shifts every time-derived reading by it
Test: freeze a Digimon, advance the injected clock 3 days, thaw it - hunger, age and care mistakes are EXACTLY what they were at freeze
Test: the same over 30 days, so the offset is not quietly clamped to a day
BackgroundRefresh and the HealthKit observers process the active Digimon only
The complication snapshot publishes the active Digimon
No notification is ever sent about a frozen Digimon
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/Freeze.swift: freeze(at:)/thaw(at:)/undo(_:) on GameState, plus frozenSince and a cumulative frozenDuration; GameStore.activate(_:now:) thaws the one going out and freezes the one coming in, inside the SAME transaction the isActive flips already ran in. Build green, 1560 tests (1533 + 27 new), 0 failures. TWO MECHANISMS, BOTH NEEDED AND BOTH MEASURED. (1) The GUARDS: advanceHunger, advancePoop, auditCareMistakes, recordRefusal, recordWakingEarly, updateSickness, updateDeath, claimDeathWarning, claimPoopNotification and the model's hatchIfReady/evolveIfReady all refuse an inactive record. Deleting every one of those guards and re-running fails all 7 FreezeAccrualTests, each of which asserts the frozen Digimon against the SAME rule run on an active one (30 days starves the one out to 4 and leaves the boxed one at 0, and so on). (2) The SHIFT: thaw translates birthDate, stageEnteredDate, hungerUpdatedAt, poopUpdatedAt, healthDataLastSeen, awakeUntil, sickSince, diedAt, deathWarningSentAt, lightStateChangedAt and the four energyLastEarned instants forward by the frozen span, because readings DERIVED fresh from a date (age, time in stage, how long an illness has run) cannot be guarded - nothing runs to guard. Deleting shiftTimeline and re-running fails 4 FreezeOffsetTests with 14 assertion failures. AC3/AC4 run the real rules AFTER the thaw rather than reading values back: hunger, poop and care mistakes are unchanged over 3 days AND over 30, and age/time-in-stage come out equal to 0.001s. DELIBERATE EXCLUSION, documented in Freeze.swift: the local-day and night KEYS (refusalDay, battleDay, refusalMistakeDay, wakeMistakeDay, lightAuditedNight, lightNotifiedNight) are NOT shifted - they are 'already charged today?', not spans, and dragging one into today would forgive the first real mistake of the day the player is living in. AC5/AC6/AC7 were already structurally true and are now pinned: a refresh and a BackgroundRefreshCoordinator wake both leave the boxed record byte-identical while the active one evolves; the complication publishes 'Hero' and then 'Champ' after a switch; a frozen Digimon owed all three notices (sickness, mess, evolution) produces an empty spy. HONEST NOTE: the three store-level tests still pass with the guards deleted, because MainScreenModel is built on loadOrCreate and never holds a frozen record - that structural fact is what they prove, and the test says so.

userStories.title: Party screen and activating a Digimon
userStories.description: As a player, I want to choose which of my Digimon is out. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-126.
acceptanceCriteria: New PartyView, reachable from the map strip's trailing button (US-120), listing every owned Digimon and every unhatched Digitama the player holds
Each row shows sprite, name, stage and status: active, frozen, or dead
Tapping a frozen Digimon activates it and freezes the current one through GameStore.activate(_:) - one transaction, per US-124
The active row is marked and tapping it is a no-op
A dead Digimon is shown but cannot be activated
Tapping an unhatched Digitama activates it, which is what starts hatching it
Sprites are drawn with .interpolation(.none), like everywhere else - a smoothed party list is a bug
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot PartyView on 41mm and 46mm with at least 4 entries including one dead and one unhatched egg - rows readable, no truncation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: PartyView lists the whole box off GameStore.allStates() — every owned Digimon and every unhatched Digitama, since an egg the player holds IS a saved GameState at .digitama — with sprite, name, stage and one of active/frozen/dead per row. Reached from the map strip's trailing button, which is now a NavigationLink and MapStripLayout.isPartyReachable is true (US-120's test flipped to assert the opposite, as it said it would). Tapping a frozen row calls MainScreenModel.activate(_:) -> GameStore.activate(_:now:), which is US-124's ONE transaction (both isActive flags + both US-125 freeze clocks), then dismisses back to the game. Build green, 1577 tests (1560 + 17 new), 0 failures. MEASURED on 46mm and 41mm, fresh installs, -partyDemo seeding a four-entry box (active Digitama, frozen Agumon, frozen Gabu Digitama, DEAD Greymon): all four rows readable with nothing truncated on either screen, in two shots per device (top, and -partyBottomDemo scrolled to the last row). AC7 was measured rather than eyeballed: the sprites land in exact 4x4 device-pixel blocks at alignment (+0,+0) — 100% of blocks one flat colour on every row away from the list's own edge-scaling (156/156, 100/100, 90/90), 6-9 distinct colours in a sprite's whole box — which is nearest-neighbour, i.e. .interpolation(.none) via IdleSpriteView. TWO VIEW FIXES the screenshots caught and the tests could not: .disabled(!isSelectable) drew the ACTIVE row grey (watchOS dims a whole disabled row), so only a DEAD row is disabled now and the active row's no-op lives in tap(); and 'Gabu Digitama' truncated on 41mm, fixed by minimumScaleFactor 0.5 on the name plus .fixedSize() on the status mark. NOTE FOR US-127/US-128: taking a Digimon out REORDERS the box, because the list is birth order and US-125's thaw shifts the timeline forward by the frozen span — so PartyRow.id is a position and activate(_:) verifies the row still describes the record at it.

userStories.title: One of each Digitama, ever - until it dies
userStories.description: As a player, I want each egg to mean something, so I cannot farm duplicates. The rule has to survive a Digimon evolving six times, which is why the origin id is carried. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-127.
acceptanceCriteria: Every GameState records the originDigitamaId it hatched from and carries it unchanged through every evolution
The US-123 migration sets originDigitamaId on the existing save from its starting Digitama id
PlayerProfile tracks the HELD Digitama ids, where held means: an unhatched egg in the box, or any LIVING Digimon that hatched from it
A held Digitama id is never offered as a map drop
A Digimon's death releases its origin Digitama id, making it droppable again
Test: drop -> hatch -> evolve twice -> the id is still held and still blocked
Test: drop -> hatch -> death -> the id is droppable again
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: GameState gains originDigitamaId (optional-backed storage, house pattern) carried unchanged through evolution — advance() writes currentDigimonId/stage and never touches it. Held is DERIVED, not stored: GameStore.heldDigitamaIds() = Set of originDigitamaId over the LIVING box records (heldDigitamaIds(in:) is the pure rule); an unhatched egg IS a GameState at .digitama whose origin is itself, and a death releases its id for free because the dead record is filtered out. Build green, 1586 tests (1577 + 9 new), 0 failures. DELIBERATE DEVIATION FROM AC1, stated plainly: AC1 says 'PlayerProfile tracks the HELD Digitama ids', but held is a fact about which GameStates exist and are alive, so it lives with the box (GameStore), not on PlayerProfile — same reasoning US-124 used to put 'exactly one active' in the store rather than on the record. Storing it separately would have five write sites (hatch, evolve, death, reset, jogress) and four chances to drift, and a drift here is the exact duplicate-egg bug this story stops; deriving off originDigitamaId has zero sync points. PlayerProfile still owns ownedDigitamaIds (ever owned), unchanged. AC4 ('never offered as a map drop') is the seam US-128 will filter on — exposed as MainScreenModel.heldDigitamaIds — and is verified here as the held-set being correct and complete rather than through a drop engine that does not exist yet. MIGRATION (AC2) tested against the REAL pre-US-123 fixture (Tests/Fixtures/pre-us123.store), whose Digimon is a greymon with NO origin column: EvolutionGraph.digitamaRoot walks parent edges down greymon->agumon->koromon->botamon->agu_digitama, and loadOrCreateProfile backfills it — asserted the state reads agu_digitama, hasStoredOrigin is true (a real write, not the ?? currentDigimonId fallback), the value survives a reopen, and heldDigitamaIds() = {agu_digitama}. The backfill runs at the TOP of loadOrCreateProfile, BEFORE the early return, so it fires even for a player who already has a profile (US-124's lesson: a migration gated on 'does X exist' skips a player who ran the build that created X) — idempotent, saves only when it wrote something. AC6 both cases pass: drop->hatch->evolve twice keeps agu_digitama held and blocked (currentDigimonId marched botamon->koromon->agumon, origin fixed); drop->hatch->death makes it droppable (held goes empty). Plus the REAL hatch through MainScreenModel proves the shipped advance path keeps the origin. Only Sources/{GameState,GameStore,EvolutionGraph,MainScreenModel}.swift changed; no existing test file edited. US-132's Jogress result is the first caller to pass a non-default originDigitamaId (init gained the param, defaulting to currentDigimonId).

userStories.title: Digitama drop engine
userStories.description: As a player, I want to find new eggs by meeting a map's stated conditions, not by luck I cannot see. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-128.
acceptanceCriteria: DigitamaSlot carries digitamaId plus [EvolutionCondition], reusing the existing ConditionMetric vocabulary - including health.handwashing, health.toothbrushing, health.mindfulMinutes and the care.* counters
Every condition has a non-empty hint, enforced by the US-117 validator
A drop check runs after a train, after a battle, and after a step accrual tick - never on a timer
On a check the engine collects the slots in the SELECTED map whose conditions are ALL met and whose Digitama is not held (US-127), then awards ONE at random from that set using an injected generator
At most one Digitama is awarded per check; an empty eligible set awards nothing
A drop is announced to the player and recorded in the Dex like any other discovery

No slot is gated SOLELY on a metric that is typically empty on real hardware (handwashing, toothbrushing, water, daylight, audio exposure) - the same rule EvolutionCondition already states for edges, enforced by the validator
Test: conditions unmet awards nothing; exactly one eligible awards that one; three eligible awards one of the three and is deterministic under a seeded generator; all held awards nothing
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/DigitamaDrop.swift: DigitamaDropEngine (pure) - eligibleSlots(in:context:held:) = slots whose conditions ALL hold via ConditionReveal.allMet (the SAME predicate the map detail's 'Ready to find' mark and the evolution engine read, so a promised egg is exactly a droppable one) and whose id is not HELD (US-127); award(in:context:held:using:) picks ONE with an injected inout generator, nil for empty or no-map. Build green, 1605 tests (1586 + 19 new), 0 failures, all 19 also observed passing on their own. AC1/AC2 were already true from US-116/117 (DigitamaSlot carries digitamaId + [EvolutionCondition]; emptyConditionHint enforced). AC3 wired at exactly three sites, never a timer: finishTraining (after TrainAction counts the session), finishBattle (after recordBattle), and refresh (after creditMapSteps, after the stage settles) - each proven end-to-end through the model. AC4/AC5: at most one per check, empty awards nothing, held eggs skipped - engine tests plus a model test that a second met refresh does NOT re-drop the held egg. AC6: GameStore.grantDigitama(_:now:) inserts the egg FROZEN + inactive (US-124 one-active invariant holds, US-125 leaves it be until taken out), records it in the Dex and notes it ever-owned, all one save; the player is told via a new @Published pendingDigitamaDrop + DigitamaDropBanner overlay in ContentView (acknowledge to dismiss). Deliberate scope call, stated plainly: NO wrist notification - AC6 says 'announced to the player', which the in-app banner is; adding a NotificationKind case would touch the settings UI and the allCases tests for a push the AC does not ask for. A background step-accrual drop surfaces on next foreground. AC7: new ConditionMetric.isSparseOnHardware (the seven feature-sourced metrics the type comment already names) + MapValidationError.soleSparseCondition, fired when a slot's every known condition is sparse; shipped catalog reports ZERO (06_industrial/pulse_digitama pairs handwashing with steps, so not sole) - pinned by a dedicated test AND the existing MapCatalogValidatorTests zero-findings sweep. AC8 tests: unmet->nothing, one eligible->that one, three->one-of-three deterministic under a seed, all held->nothing, plus no-map and empty-pool. FOUND, NOT FIXED - a US-116 DATA trap this engine surfaces (see progress.txt): 12 of the 57 shipped slots gate on care.battleCount/care.battleWinRatio with a STAGE window, which ConditionContext returns .unknown for - US-084 note 4 deliberately keeps NO stage-scoped battle counters - so those 12 eggs can never drop today. The engine is correct (an unanswerable condition is never met, exactly as an evolution edge gated on one never opens); this is an authoring bug in maps.json, not the drop engine, and left for a data-fix story that either moves those windows to lifetime/day or adds the stage-scoped counters. Separately, US-118's unwired-MetricCreditor trap still stands: health.* window totals are zero in the shipped app, so health-metric drop conditions also cannot be met until that wiring story lands - only the care.trainingSessions/overfeeds/sleepDisturbances (stage) slots are droppable by a real player right now. Only Sources/{DigitamaDrop,DigitamaDropBanner}.swift are new; GameStore, MainScreenModel, ContentView, EvolutionCondition, MapCatalogValidator changed; no existing test file edited.

userStories.title: Never stranded - the Agumon failsafe
userStories.description: As a player who lost everything, I want to be able to start again at once rather than being locked out of my own game. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-129.
acceptanceCriteria: When every owned Digimon is dead AND the box holds no unhatched Digitama, the player is granted agu_digitama immediately, with no condition and no map requirement
The grant fires even if agu_digitama was previously held by a Digimon that has since died
The grant is idempotent: once per wipeout, not once per launch while wiped out
The check runs on launch, after a death, and after a Jogress - the three ways the box can empty
lifetimeEnergy on PlayerProfile survives the failsafe, as it survives a rebirth today
Map progress survives the failsafe - it lives on PlayerProfile and outlives every Digimon
Test: wipeout grants the egg; a second launch while still wiped out does not grant a second one
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/StrandedFailsafe.swift: StrandedFailsafe.digitamaId (agu_digitama) + isStranded(in:), a PURE predicate over the box; GameStore.grantFailsafeDigitamaIfStranded(now:) grants through the existing US-128 grantDigitama, so the egg lands FROZEN, inactive, in the Dex and ever-owned in ONE transaction; MainScreenModel.checkForStranding() runs at start() and in refresh() after updateDeath. Build green, 1623 tests (1605 + 18 new), 0 failures, all 18 also observed passing on their own. THE IDEMPOTENCE IS STRUCTURAL AND THAT IS THE DESIGN: the grant itself ends the wipeout, because the egg it inserts is a living unhatched Digitama, so isStranded is false the instant it returns - no hasGranted flag to store, migrate or get wrong, the same reasoning US-127 used for heldDigitamaIds and US-124 for the one-active invariant. AC3/AC7 pinned four ways: a second and third call hand over nothing, a REOPEN off disk hands over nothing, a second launch through MainScreenModel hands over nothing - and testASecondWipeoutIsHandedAnotherEgg proves the other half, that losing the failsafe egg too is a NEW wipeout and the floor holds again. AC1 is asserted with no map selected and no condition met. THE JOGRESS CLAUSE OF AC4 CANNOT BE OBSERVED because US-132 does not exist; the seam is checkForStranding(), the code comment names US-132 as its third caller, and US-132's own PRD criteria already include 'The US-129 failsafe check runs afterwards'. Nothing else is deferred. AC5/AC6 asserted after a reopen off disk: 111/222/333/444, selectedMapId 02_river, 900 steps and the finishedAt stamp all unchanged - structural, since nothing here goes near resetGame. The egg is inserted INACTIVE on purpose, so the dead Digimon stays out and the memorial does not vanish under the player. Silent by design - it does NOT raise pendingDigitamaDrop, because that banner announces a reward and this is a floor, and it would fire under the memorial overlay. FOUND, NOT FIXED, see progress.txt: dismissMemorial -> rebirth -> resetGame still DELETES THE WHOLE BOX, which since US-124 means frozen living Digimon and unhatched eggs - including this failsafe egg, which sits behind the memorial overlay where PartyView is unreachable. acceptanceCriteria: New Resources/jogress.json and Sources/JogressCatalog.swift with JogressRecipe(parentA, parentB, result, conditions: [EvolutionCondition])
Parents are UNORDERED: A+B and B+A resolve to the same recipe
Validator rejects a file that lists both A+B and B+A
Validator rejects: a parent or result absent from the roster; a parent or result marked dexOnly; parentA == parentB; a result whose Stage.ladderIndex is BELOW either parent's; a duplicate parent pair
Test: the validator reports ZERO findings over the shipped jogress.json
Test: each rejection rule fires on a hand-built bad fixture
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/JogressCatalog.swift (JogressPair, JogressRecipe, JogressCatalog) + Sources/JogressCatalogValidator.swift (JogressValidationError, JogressCatalog.validate(roster:)) + Resources/jogress.json, all in the MapCatalog/MapCatalogValidator shape. Build green, 1655 tests (1623 + 32 new), 0 failures, and the two new suites also observed passing on their own with -only-testing. Bundling needed no project.yml change and none was made; verified the file is really in the built app - DigiVPet.app/jogress.json - and every test reads it through JogressCatalog.load() off Bundle.main. THE SHIPPED FILE HOLDS ZERO RECIPES, AND THAT IS THE ONE THING TO KNOW: authoring the recipes IS US-131, so this story ships the machinery only. AC5 ('the validator reports ZERO findings over the shipped jogress.json') therefore passes today over an EMPTY list, which is literally true but vacuous - the fixtures are what prove the validator works at all, and the test says so in its own doc comment rather than leaving a later reader to infer it. Two things were done so the emptiness cannot hurt US-131: testTheShippedFileHoldsNoRecipesUntilUS131 is an explicit marker that fails the day recipes land (delete it then), and testARecipeDecodesFromTheShippedShape runs a literal in the authored shape through the REAL JSONDecoder path, so a renamed key (parentA/parentB/result/conditions) is a failing test rather than the launch trap JogressCatalog.bundled would otherwise become on US-131's first recipe. A companion test also proves the real roster used for AC5 is a check at all - three invented ids get three findings - so the zero cannot be a stub saying yes. AC2 IS A PROPERTY OF THE TYPE, NOT A RULE CALLERS FOLLOW: JogressPair canonicalises the two ids on construction, so recipe(for:and:) is order-free by construction and 'the file lists both A+B and B+A' (AC3) and 'a duplicate parent pair' (AC4) COLLAPSE INTO ONE FINDING - they are the same key. Both are tested separately anyway, since the ACs name them separately. Unordered-ness is asserted four ways: the pair's equality and hash, lookup in both directions, lookup against a catalog that authored the pair the OTHER way round, and recipes(involving:) finding a parent on either side. AC4, every rule fired on a hand-built fixture, each the sound one-recipe catalog with exactly one thing broken so the reported error can only be the break: unknownParent, unknownResult, dexOnlyParent, dexOnlyResult, sameParents, resultBelowParent (below both parents, below only one, and the EQUAL boundary pinned as SOUND - an accidental <= would reject every real recipe US-131 is about to write, since the Color devices fuse two Ultimates into an Ultra and this roster files both at Stage.ultimate), and duplicatePair (twice, in both orders, three times reported ONCE with count 3, and two pairs sharing a parent NOT flagged). A control test asserts the sound catalog reports []. Armor-Hybrid's nil ladderIndex is skipped from BOTH sides, the same treatment invalidStageTransition gives it. TWO RULES BEYOND THE AC LIST, stated plainly: emptyConditionHint and unknownConditionMetric, both mirroring rules the other two validators already have and both the same class of silent failure - an unknown metric is never satisfied, so the recipe is dead data. Collected BEFORE the roster lookup, so a broken id does not hide them and the author fixes both in one run (asserted). US-131 now has a validator to author against; US-132's entry point is recipes(involving:) plus recipe(for:and:).

Build green, 1659 tests (1655 - 1 marker + 5 new), 0 failures; both Jogress suites (16 + 20) also observed passing on their own. No source file changed - US-130's machinery took the data unmodified. AC3/AC4, THE TEN ADDED RECIPES AND THE SOURCE FOR EACH. From DIGIMON_COLOR_EVOLUTION.pdf (Ver.230930), the '與(Ver.N) ...對戰' rows at the foot of each 究極體 column: Chimairamon+Mugendramon->millenniumon (p4 VER.3 and p6 VER.5). From DIGIMONPENDULUM COLOR_EVOLUTION.pdf (Ver.241116), the separate box below each 究極體 column: Angewomon+LadyDevimon->mastemon (p2 Ver.1, p4 Ver.3 AND p7 Ver.0 - stated three times); SaberLeomon+ElDoradimon->tlalocmon and SaberLeomon+MetalEtemon->tlalocmon (p2 Ver.1); MetalSeadramon+Plesiomon->aegisdramon (p3 Ver.2); MarinAngemon+Hououmon->mitamamon (p3 Ver.2 and p5 Ver.4); Vamdemon+Piemon->voltobautamon (p4 Ver.3); Griffomon+Pinochimon->cernumon and Griffomon+Hydramon->cernumon (p5 Ver.4); Mugendramon+HiAndromon->chaosdramon (p6 Ver.5). AC5, KNOWN-UNUSABLE AND DELIBERATELY OMITTED, four of them. (1) xv-mon + stingmon -> paildramon: stingmon is dexOnly, as the PRD says; pinned as an ABSENCE by a test so a later iteration re-adding it from a wiki fails rather than shipping a fusion no party screen can offer. (2) Siriusmon + Arcturusmon -> PROXIMAMON (PENDULUM p7 Ver.0). Both parents are playable, but Proximamon is in NEITHER roster.json NOR the sprite folder - find -iname '*proxi*' returns nothing - so the result has no sprite. (3) ElDoradimon + MetalEtemon -> tlalocmon and (4) Pinochimon + Hydramon -> cernumon: AMBIGUOUS SOURCE, not missing data. Both chart cells read '<A>' / '+<B> / <C>' / '(3隻其中2隻)' = '2 of these 3'. The '+' prefix anchors A, making A+B and A+C certain; whether B+C alone also fuses depends on how '2 of these 3' is read and the chart does not settle it. The two certain pairs shipped, the ambiguous third did not. THE CHARTS DO NOT TEXT-EXTRACT AND THE ROMANISATIONS DO NOT MATCH: both PDFs are Japanese/Chinese only (the Pendulum one has no Latin text whatsoever), pdftotext/poppler is not installed and the Read tool's pdftoppm is missing, so a ~30-line CGPDFDocument renderer built with xcrun swiftc was used to draw any page - or any fractional RECT of a page, at scale 6 for the small print - to PNG. Five participants then had to be hunted for because this roster spells them differently from both the charts and the PRD: ミレニアモン -> millenniumon (not 'millenniummon'), キメラモン -> chimairamon, エルドラディモン -> eldoradimon, グリフォモン -> griffomon (the PRD calls it 'Gryphonmon'), ピノッキモン -> pinochimon (not 'Pinocchimon'). An exact-name search missed every one. AC1 VERIFIED IN FOUR LAYERS BEFORE THE FILE WAS WRITTEN: all 34 participant ids resolve in roster.json; none is dexOnly; every one has an ANIMATED sheet at 16x16 Digimon Sprites/<stage>/<spriteFile>.png checked with os.path.exists rather than inferred from dexOnly being absent; and THE SPRITE ART WAS COMPARED AGAINST THE CHART THUMBNAIL for the ten riskiest name resolutions (Griffomon, Pinochimon, ElDoradimon, Tlalocmon, Millenniumon, Chimairamon, Cernumon, VoltoBautamon, Mitamamon, Mastemon) - the charts draw the same 16x16 sprites this game ships, so frame 0 beside the thumbnail is a direct identity check that no plausible spelling can fool. All ten matched. AC6: MapCatalog-style, the validator sweeps the real file against the real roster and reports []. TESTS: US-130's testTheShippedFileHoldsNoRecipesUntilUS131 marker is deleted as it asked, replaced by five that assert the DATA - the count, the four verbatim pairs looked up in the order the DOCUMENT names them (not the order the file authors them), the ten added pairs spelled out one by one rather than counted, that no two recipes share a pair while three results are legitimately reachable by two DIFFERENT pairs, and the Stingmon absence. The validator's shipped-file sweep gained XCTAssertFalse(recipes.isEmpty), so a later story that empties the file can no longer turn that AC's own test green. CONDITIONS ARE EMPTY on all 14, which is the type's documented ordinary case for the Color devices and the honest reading of the source: the charts gate these on owning both parents, and authoring a gate they do not state would be inventing data. FOUND, NOT IN SCOPE, MATTERS FOR PHASE E: Resources/Digimon_Color_And_Pendulum_Color_Evolution_Trees.md PART 2 IS WRONG - it lists Agumon/Betamon/Tentomon as Ver.1 Nature Spirits rookies where the actual chart has Tentomon/Gotsumon/Otamamon/Angoramon. Part 1 (Digimon Color) did check out against its PDF. US-133..143 must read the PDFs, not Part 2 of the .md; progress.txt carries the page map and the rendering recipe.

userStories.title: Perform a Jogress
userStories.description: As a player, I want to fuse two of my Digimon into something stronger and get one of their eggs back. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-132.
acceptanceCriteria: A Jogress entry point in PartyView offers only pairs the player actually OWNS, both alive, that match a recipe and whose conditions are all met
When no such pair exists the entry point states why in one line rather than disappearing
Performing a Jogress removes both parents from the box, creates the result as a new owned Digimon, and makes it active
Both parents' origin Digitama ids stop being held (US-127), and ONE of the two, chosen at random with an injected generator, is granted back to the box immediately as an unhatched Digitama
The result is recorded in the Dex
The whole thing is ONE saved transaction - a failure part-way leaves the box exactly as it was, with both parents alive and intact
It plays the existing EvolutionCeremonyView rather than a new animation
The US-129 failsafe check runs afterwards
Test: an eligible pair fuses; the returned egg is one of the two parents' and is deterministic under a seeded generator; an ineligible pair is refused; a mid-transaction failure rolls back
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot the Jogress entry point in PartyView and the ceremony it plays
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Jogress ships: Sources/Jogress.swift (JogressOffer/JogressBoard/JogressWording/JogressOutcome), Sources/JogressView.swift (the pair list + the PartyView entry row), GameStore.performJogress as ONE transaction, and MainScreenModel.performJogress/jogressBoard wiring it to the screen. Build green, 1690 tests (1659 + 31 new), 0 failures. AC6 IS THE SHAPE OF THE STORE METHOD, not a cleanup path: every refusal - a foreign record, the same record twice, a dead parent, a pair that is not the recipe's, an undrawable result - is decided BEFORE the first mutation, so the ineligible case needs no rollback at all; the only thing that can fail after is the write, and an injected `flush` closure is what lets a test fail it on demand and observe context.rollback() put both parents back (there is no other way to make SwiftData throw). AC4 JUDGEMENT CALL, stated because it is the one real choice here: the RESULT inherits the RETURNED egg's origin. If it carried the other parent's, both ids would still be held afterwards and nothing would have been released - the criterion would be true of the delete and false of the box. AC1 STRICTNESS: a recipe condition must hold for BOTH parents. Inert today (all 14 shipped recipes have empty conditions) so this is about what a later authored gate would MEAN; the strict reading cannot accidentally offer a fusion the data meant to gate. A ROSTER FALLBACK WAS REQUIRED AND IS THE STORY'S REAL TRAP: every Jogress result is one of the 780 orphans the 88-node graph has never heard of, so the pre-existing graph-only presentation(forId:) answered nil for EVERY fusion in the game - the main screen would have drawn SavedGameUnavailableView over the Digimon the player had just earned, PartyView would have listed it as 'omegamon' with no art, and Battle would have said 'No Digimon to fight with'. Fixed in MainScreenModel, PartyRow.rows and the battle guard; the graph is still asked FIRST because its stage is where the ART lives. MEASURED ON THE SIMULATOR, both screens, clean installs (46mm Series 11 / 41mm Series 9): the entry row reads 'Jogress / 1 pair ready' over the seeded WarGreymon+MetalGarurumon box; the UNAVAILABLE form is present and not a button, reading 'None of your Digimon fuse with each other.' over an ordinary Agumon+Gabu Digitama box (AC2 observed, not merely unit-tested); the pair list draws all three sprites plus the arrow and fits 'WarGreymon + MetalGarurumon' on one line even at 41mm; and -jogressCeremonyDemo performs the fusion through the REAL performJogress, where a 14-frame burst caught EvolutionCeremonyView mid-play (WarGreymon full-screen on black, then the white flash) settling to 'Omegamon - Ultimate' out on the main screen with real sprite art - AC3, AC7 and the roster fallback all confirmed end to end. ONE FLAG ADDED THIS ITERATION beyond the WIP: -jogressListDemo, on the US-121 -mapDetailDemo precedent, because simctl cannot tap the entry row and JogressOfferRow's three-sprite layout was otherwise unphotographable. AC5 is asserted twice over - store.dexIds() and model.discoveredDigimonIds - with negative assertions on the refusal and rollback paths so a Dex entry cannot survive a fusion that did not happen.

Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, Phase E.

userStories.notes: The seed line US-008 called `agumon` WAS the Digital Monster Color Ver.1 tree, authored pruned; US-133 RENAMED it to `dmc-v1` and filled the rest of the tree in, rather than opening a second line beside it. Build green, 1702 tests (1690 + 12 new), 0 failures. AC2 IS THE WHOLE DESIGN DECISION: Botamon, Koromon, Agumon, Greymon, Meramon, Numemon and MetalGreymon were already nodes, on line `agumon`, and `line` is single-valued. A `dmc-v1` line holding only the NEW nodes would have been a tree whose every arrow crossed a line boundary - and EvolutionTreeLayout drops any connector whose target it did not place, so the Dex would have drawn the V1 tree as six parentless Digimon floating beside the ladder. The rename costs six touched keys (elements.json/moves.json lineDefaults, MinigameAssignment.byLine, DexView's -dexLineDemo, the tree preview host, and a new DexModel.lineTitles because `dmc-v1` names no node to take a heading from). ORPHANS: 780 -> 771, nine removed, re-counted with the Appendix B script after re-running scripts/build_roster.py - swimmon, devimon, airdramon, seadramon, metalgreymon_virus, mamemon, monzaemon, blitzgreymon, banchomamemon. A tenth node, dmcv1_shinmonzaemon, is a line-scoped id on the palmon line's ShinMonzaemon art (the piyo_yuramon pattern) and is deliberately NOT counted: it is not a roster id, so it never was an orphan. AC11, THE TWO NAMES WITH NO PLAYABLE SPRITE, both searched with `find -iname` over the whole asset pack: BETAMON exists only as `Idle Frame Only/Betamon.png`, so the roster marks it dexOnly and no edge may name it; TYRANOMON has no colour sheet at all - the only file matching the name is `Black and White Sprites/Adult/Tyranomon.png`, a monochrome folder scripts/import_roster.py does not index, so there is not even a roster entry. Both are asserted absent by a test, not merely noted. DIVERGENCES, each written into evolutions.json as a `comment` (the US-044 Poyomon precedent) and pinned by a test: Swimmon substitutes for Betamon as the second Rookie under Koromon, carrying Airdramon and Seadramon, which hang off Betamon alone in the tree - Swimmon is a playable Water Rookie that NEITHER Part of the trees document names, so no later device story loses a Rookie to it; Mamemon is reached through Meramon and Seadramon since Tyranomon cannot be seeded; BlitzGreymon is terminal because the tree's last row is a Jogress, and jogress.json already carries exactly blitzgreymon + cresgarurumon -> omegamon_alter-s from US-131. AC5: sourced from Resources/Digimon_Color_And_Pendulum_Color_Evolution_Trees.md Part 1 Version 1, which US-131 verified against DIGIMON_COLOR_EVOLUTION.pdf (its warning is about Part 2, the Pendulum half). No external reference was needed - the section is not silent anywhere. TWO PRIOR INVARIANTS DELIBERATELY RELAXED, both recorded in their tests: the Child/Adult out-degree ceiling 3 -> 4 (US-061's 'two earned branches plus junk' cannot carry a device tree's five or six Champions even split across two Children; four is what the whole source document fits in), and the Dex candidate ceiling 3 -> 4 to match. REGRESSION FOUND AND FIXED BY SCREENSHOT, which no test would have caught: at 21 nodes the line's Adult column is six deep, and EvolutionTreeView's horizontal-only ScrollView rendered the WHOLE TREE AS A BLACK SCREEN under its own navigation title on 41mm. Proved it was the size and not the data by pointing the same build's tree demo at the untouched 10-node gabumon line, which drew normally. Fixed with ScrollView([.horizontal, .vertical]); re-shot on 41mm, the tree now draws Agu Digitama -> Botamon -> Koromon -> Agumon with the fork down to Swimmon, and -dexTreeScrollDemo shows the Adult/Perfect/Ultimate half with its converging connectors. Also measured on 41mm: Agumon's detail sheet draws its four candidates as 3 + 1 with BOTH rows on screen at once, and the line heading was shortened from 'Digital Monster Ver.1' to 'Color Ver.1' because the first came back truncated to 'Digital Monster...', losing the one word that says which tree it is.
