userStories.notes: The seed line US-008 called `gabumon` WAS the Digital Monster Color Ver.2 tree, authored pruned; US-134 RENAMED it to `dmc-v2` and filled the rest of the tree in - the same shape as US-133, exactly as that story predicted. Build green, 1716 tests (1702 + 14 new), 0 failures, the 14 also observed passing on their own; the total was cross-checked against `grep -rcE '^\s+func test' Tests/*.swift` = 1716, so no crashed host inflated it. ORPHANS 771 -> 760, ELEVEN removed, re-counted with the Appendix B script after re-running scripts/build_roster.py: elecmon, kabuterimon, angemon, yukidarumon, vegimon, birdramon, skullgreymon, metalmamemon, whamon, skullmammon, cresgarurumon. A test names the same eleven and asserts each has an in-edge. AC11 HAS NO ENTRY, and that is the finding: EVERY name in the Version 2 section has a playable 48x64 sheet - all seventeen ls'd before being named - unlike V1's Betamon and Tyranomon. A test asserts each of the seventeen is a non-dexOnly roster entry, so this stays true rather than being a claim in a note. THREE DIVERGENCES, each written into evolutions.json as a `comment` (the US-044 precedent) and each pinned by a test. (1) WHAMON IS A PERFECT, not the document's Champion: its only animated sheet is Perfect/Whamon.png, so an Adult node would resolve to NO ART and fail the validator's missingSprite. It stands beside MetalMamemon rather than beneath it, off the same two Champions the document puts on its row (Garurumon, Yukidarumon), and shares MetalMamemon's Mega - the one invented arrow, needed because a terminal Perfect is a Digimon that can never evolve again, and no Perfect in the file is terminal today. Pendulum Color V2 puts Whamon at Perfect too, so US-139 will need a scoped id for it. (2) VEGIMON REPLACED GEREMON AS THE JUNK FALLBACK: a device tree's junk Champion is the one BOTH Rookies fall to (Numemon in V1, Vegimon in V2, Scumon in V3), and the document hangs Vegimon off Gabumon and Elecmon alike. Geremon would then have had no parent at all - a shipped Digimon reachable from nothing - so it is Elecmon's overfeeding branch now (care.overfeeds atLeast 6), keeping weregarurumon and gerbemon under it. SeedRosterTests' Ver.2 junk path moved to Vegimon -> Vademon -> Ebemon accordingly. (3) VEGIMON -> SKULLGREYMON is invented: the document gives Vegimon only Vademon, and an Adult with one edge is a dead end at Adult. LINE-SCOPED IDS dmcv2_vademon / dmcv2_ebemon, the dmcv1_shinmonzaemon pattern: the gazimon line already owns `vademon` and `ebemon`, `line` is single-valued and EvolutionTreeLayout drops any connector leaving its line. Neither is a roster id, so neither is counted as an orphan removed. Both needed their own elements.json and moves.json entries with signature names unique across the file. TWO CEILINGS RAISED, one step and with the reason in the test: out-degree 4 -> 5 (Gabumon's five Champions are ALL drawable, so nothing prunes it, and the document draws all five arrows out of Gabumon - splitting them across the Rookies is not open), and the Dex candidate ceiling 4 -> 5 to match. US-133's note that 'four is what the whole source document fits in' was WRONG: V4's Palmon and V5's Gizamon are six wide, so US-136/US-137 will raise it again - deliberately not pre-raised, since a ceiling should never be looser than the file it guards. So the test asserts every EARNED edge at Child-and-up is conditioned, every condition in the WHOLE file has a non-blank hint, and every junk edge is unconditioned. US-136 took the same reading. AC11's one absent name: Flymon. Also pinned: 'Machinedramon' is Mugendramon's dub name in the document's own '(Machinedramon)' and matches nothing in the pack or roster - one Digimon, not two. userStories.notes: Digitama orphans 45 -> 23 (all 23 left are L-Z, US-145's); whole-file orphans 628 -> 598, the extra 8 being the Baby I this sweep opened. 30 nodes added, 300 -> 330: 22 Digitama plus 8 Baby I. Twelve eggs join a device line their species is already wired into and add no rung (agu2006/gabublack/elec/kune/hyoko/angora/cand/beta/kame/kuda/kuda2006/espi); ten open a thread and bring their own Baby I, in five new lines - tamers, wanyamon, diablomon, commandramon, algomon. Every Wikimon arrow is cited in the node's comment. Build green, 1936 tests 0 failures, validator zero findings. TWO DEVIATIONS, both deliberate and both pinned by a test. (1) The AC 'every edge has at least one condition with a non-empty hint' is NOT met for the 22 hatch edges, and cannot be at this stage: EggHatcher.hatchTarget reads minEnergy and ignores conditions entirely, so a condition would be data that lies - and since every egg has exactly one way out, a condition an engine DID honour could leave a player's only egg unable to hatch. All 12 previously-shipped hatch edges are unconditional for the same reason; DigitamaSweepTests.testHatchEdgesAreUnconditionalAndUngatedByType states it as a claim. (2) The AC 'every Digimon in the range gets at least one in-edge' is impossible at Digitama - nothing evolves into an egg. Every one of the 22 got an out-edge, which is what the Appendix B count measures. Two invariants had to be generalised, and six earlier stories' tests with them: a line may now have SEVERAL eggs (the 'one egg per line' assertion in all six Pendulum/DMC tree tests now pins the exact egg list instead), and a node below Ultimate may be a dead end (the 8 new Baby I; ledgered by testTheOnlyDeadEndsBelowUltimateAreTheOnesThisSweepOpened for US-146/US-147 to shrink). MainScreenModel.startingDigitamaId now filters a new game's egg through EvolutionGraph.reachesUltimate so nobody starts on an unfinished thread.

userStories.notes: Digitama orphans 23 -> 0, and the stage is now CLOSED: all 57 playable eggs carry their one hatch edge. Whole-file orphans 598 -> 563, the extra 12 being the Baby I this sweep opened. 35 nodes added, 330 -> 365: 23 Digitama plus 12 Baby I. Build green, 1951 tests 0 failures (1936 + 15 new, all 15 also observed passing on their own), validator zero findings over the whole file. THE RULE US-144 SET WAS TIGHTENED, and the reason is arithmetic rather than taste: `EggHatcher.hatchTarget` reads `evolutions.first`, so an egg has exactly one hatch edge and a Baby I's only possible in-edge is an egg of its own - and after this story there are no eggs left. So every egg spent on an already-reachable Baby I is a Baby I nobody can ever hatch. US-144's rule (an egg whose species is already wired joins that species' line) becomes: an egg opens the Baby I Wikimon puts two rungs below its species, and doubles up ONLY when it has no choice. Twelve opened one; eleven doubled up, each for one of five checked reasons (species already wired 4, species idle-only 3, species has no sheet at all 1 (Liollmon), the species' OWN Baby I idle-only 1 (Meicoomon), every candidate Baby I already in the graph 2 (Phascomon, Vorvomon)). TWELVE IS THE CEILING, PROVED NOT ASSUMED: only 13 of the 25 orphaned Baby I sit on a Wikimon thread reaching any of these 23 species at all, and two of those 13 (Leafmon, Pururumon) are reachable only through `worm_digitama`, which can hatch into one. So 13 Baby I are beyond Phase E's reach through Digitama; the number is asserted by `testTheBabyIStillOrphanedCannotBeReachedByAnyRemainingEgg` together with 'no unspent egg exists', so US-146 is told rather than having to discover it. ONLY TWO NEW LINES, not twelve: `adventure02` (V-mon, Wormmon) and `vital` (Ludomon, Zubamon, Morphomon, Pulsemon, Sunarizamon - every one of them drawn on a Vital Bracelet device, checked on the Wikimon Virtual Pets section rather than asserted). The other ten threads went onto lines that already exist: four Tamers partners (Lopmon, Monodramon, Renamon, Terriermon) onto `tamers`, which is now 15 nodes, and `pipimon`/`tsubumon` onto `palmon`/`dmc-v3` BY THEIR BABY II - Tanemon and Tokomon are already those lines', so US-147 closes those two dead ends with an edge and no new node. That pairing is pinned by `testTwoOfTheOpenedBabyIAlreadyHaveTheirBabyIIOnTheSameLine`. Every thread cites Wikimon AND names the middle rung the pairing rests on, and a test greps for both - a citation that skips the Baby II is unfalsifiable. THE DEAD-END LEDGER MOVED, deliberately: it is now the whole-file list of 20 in `DigitamaSweepLToZTests.testTheOnlyDeadEndsBelowUltimateAreTheOnesTheTwoDigitamaSweepsOpened`, because the next sweep will look at the newest sweep's file. US-144's version was narrowed to 'my eight are still dead ends' and points at the new home. US-144's marker test for this story fired as designed and was rewritten into the claim it guarded (zero orphaned Digitama, 57 wired). KNOCK-ON: nine test files, all count/egg-list pins - EvolutionGraphTests' per-line dict, graph.nodes.count in ElementCatalogTests and EvolutionGraphValidatorTests, EggHatchingTests' starting-egg split (8 new candidates, 15 new unraisable, computed not guessed), MinigameAssignmentTests' sharer map, EvolutionTreeTests' dmc-v1 layout, and the four Pendulum tree tests whose lines took an egg. PendulumWindGuardiansTreeTests' reachability was still seeded from one egg and now seeds from every egg of the line, which is the generalisation US-144 made everywhere else and missed there. TWO ACs NOT MET LITERALLY, both for the reasons US-144 recorded and both stated as tests rather than prose: nothing evolves INTO a Digitama, so 'every Digimon in the range gets an in-edge' is impossible at this stage; and a hatch edge carries no conditions, because `EggHatcher` ignores them and a condition an engine DID honour could leave a player's only egg unable to hatch. FOR US-147: `ketomon`'s only Wikimon Baby II is Hopmon, which is idle-only, so it needs a stand-in - the same problem `bommon` has. Noted in the node's own comment.

userStories.notes: Wired the M-Z third of the Child rung and FINISHED the rung: 51 new nodes (the 18 orphaned Children M-Z, 33 new Champions above them), 18 new branch edges across 14 Baby II, and out-edges on the 16 Children US-147 opened as M-Z leaves. evolutions.json 548 -> 599, plus the matching elements.json/moves.json rows, a new Tests/ChildSweepMToZTests.swift (26 tests) and 17 existing test files. NO new lines; no aliases, so new nodes and orphans removed are the same number. ORPHAN COUNT for the stage (Appendix B, regenerated roster.generated.json): Child 18 BEFORE, 0 AFTER. Whole file 381 -> 330; Adult fell 67 -> 34 as a side effect of authoring the rung above. Build green, 2057 tests, 0 failures, validator zero findings, and the 26 new tests also observed passing on their own. SCOPE READING, same as US-148/US-149: the range is the RUNG-AND-RANGE, so the 16 Children US-147 left with an in-edge and no out-edge are in it as well as the 18 orphans. THE WHOLE CHILD RUNG IS NOW COMPLETE - all 126 Child nodes, aliases included, have both edges, and that is asserted directly rather than left as three per-range claims. FOUND BY A TEST, NOT BY THE PLAN, and it is the one real bug here: `care.battleCount` is answerable ONLY over the LIFETIME window - ConditionContext.careValue returns .unknown for (careBattleCount, .stage) - so four edges first authored with "window": "stage" on a battle criterion were UNREACHABLE, not merely hard. Caught by BabyIISweepTests' engine test; every other care.* counter is the opposite way round and is .stage only. A new test pins the rule over the whole file so no later sweep repeats it. PLACEMENT DECIDED BY AN EGG'S PROMISE, per the Codebase Patterns rule: Phascomon first went to Sunmon (penc-nso) on flavour, and moving it to Kozenimon (penc-me, its cited Caprimon's line) made phasco_digitama's BFS arrive at its own species - so all three eggs that doubled up in US-145 because their species had no node (meicoo, phasco, vorvo) now reach it. Solarmon took the cited Gigimon parent on tamers to free that slot; DigitamaSweepLToZTests' "the species is still unwired" guards are replaced by the arrival check they were deferring. VARIANTS: eight of the nine hang off their base form's line and three off its own In-Training; Shakomon X could not - penc-ds has exactly ONE In-Training and Pukamon is full, the trap US-149's Gomamon X hit - so it followed its OTHER cited parent, Chocomon, onto tamers rather than being rehomed by flavour. adventure02 was the last line in the file with no Adult rung and gained NiseDrimogemon as its junk floor, which empties the one-element list US-149 left for this story. STRANDED, checked before parents were chosen: Starmon 2010 is the one Child added to EvolutionCriteriaTests' list and it is inherited - Wikimon gives it exactly one parent, Pickmon, and all three xros Baby II are unreachable because that line has no Digitama. Five Champions follow their already-stranded Child (Dorulumon, Shoutmon King, Ginryumon, Hakubamon, Parasaurmon); the other 28 sit over a reachable Child. TWO CHAMPIONS ARE NOT NEW NODES and that is a deliberate trade, the same one US-149 made for Lalamon: PetitMamon -> pencnso_devimon and Terriermon X -> Galgomon are the arrows the source draws, and both targets were already reachable, so each costs one orphan struck off in exchange for the canonical evolution. THREE HISTORICAL CLAIMS WERE REPLACED RATHER THAN BUMPED, each with the reason in the comment: US-149's "every Adult on vital/xros is one US-149 authored" (false once this story put ten more there - the junk FLOOR half survives); the Nature Spirits tree's "Koromon's only conditioned edge is the unlockable slot" (scoped to the three Rookies the SECTION draws, since YukiAgumon now hangs there too); and DexScreenTests' "the roster dwarfs the graph" (599 nodes against 1025 entries is past half, so the floor is now the 157 idle-only Digimon, which can never gain a node).

This sweep covers all 16 Armor-Hybrid, none of which is on any edge today, over whatever the Phase E device trees left behind. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, Phase E.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers every remaining orphan at stage 'Armor-Hybrid' that is still orphaned after the device-tree stories
Every Digimon in the range gets at least one in-edge (something evolves into it) and, unless it is a terminal Ultimate, at least one out-edge
Lines are grouped coherently - a sweep must NOT produce dozens of one-node lines. Variants (X-Antibody, Black, 2006, Virus) hang off their base form's line
Every edge has at least one condition with a non-empty hint; no edge is unconditional
ls every sprite before naming it; no dexOnly Digimon appears on any edge
Every new node has a line, or EvolutionGraph.bundled traps at launch
EvolutionGraphValidator reports ZERO findings over the whole of evolutions.json
notes records the orphan count for this stage BEFORE and AFTER, counted with the script in Appendix B of the PRD
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
